appsync.resolver.ApiId.Docs: The AWS AppSync GraphQL API to which you want to attach this resolver.

appsync.resolver.CachingConfig.Docs: The caching configuration for the resolver.

appsync.resolver.FieldName.Docs: The GraphQL field on a type that invokes the resolver.

appsync.resolver.Kind.Docs: The resolver type.  +  **UNIT**: A UNIT resolver type. A UNIT resolver is the default resolver type. You can use a UNIT resolver to run a GraphQL query against a single data source.+  **PIPELINE**: A PIPELINE resolver type. You can use a PIPELINE resolver to invoke a series of `Function` objects in a serial manner. You can use a pipeline resolver to run a GraphQL query against multiple data sources.
appsync.resolver.Kind.Kind` <a name=\"cfn-appsync-resolver-ki:
A UNIT resolver type\. A UNIT resolver is the default resolver type\. You can use a UNIT resolver to run a GraphQL query against a single data source\.
+  **PIPELINE*

appsync.resolver.MaxBatchSize.Docs: The maximum number of resolver request inputs that will be sent to a single AWS Lambda function in a `BatchInvoke` operation.

appsync.resolver.PipelineConfig.Docs: Functions linked with the pipeline resolver.

appsync.resolver.RequestMappingTemplate.Docs: The request mapping template.  Request mapping templates are optional when using a Lambda data source. For all other data sources, a request mapping template is required.

appsync.resolver.RequestMappingTemplateS.Docs: The location of a request mapping template in an Amazon S3 bucket. Use this if you want to provision with a template file in Amazon S3 rather than embedding it in your CloudFormation template.

appsync.resolver.ResponseMappingTemplate.Docs: The location of a response mapping template in an Amazon S3 bucket. Use this if you want to provision with a template file in Amazon S3 rather than embedding it in your CloudFormation template.

appsync.resolver.SyncConfig.Docs: The `SyncConfig` for a resolver attached to a versioned data source.

appsync.resolver.TypeName.Docs: The GraphQL type that invokes this resolver.
